what_it_does: Defines a JSON ledger of design invariants, each with an id, scope, predicate, message, and checker type, plus prose summaries of recurring visual and narrative rules.
observations: Contains a top-level description, a predicates array, and a prose array.

Predicates cover color, gold usage, surface lightness, titles, focal hierarchy, spacing, page closure, movement across boundaries, foreign hue handling, shape rarity, ordinal ramps, animation still frames, bullet semantics, narrative completeness, purpose recording, view transitions, sibling consistency, fragment portability, chain devices, anchored zones, diagram layering, proof density, nesting, chip emphasis, and comparison-grid affirmation.
Checker values include predicate, build, model, and one status-rich derived entry pattern.
Several predicates are written as natural-language or semi-formal assertions rather than directly executable expressions.
Some entries carry status metadata with dates, source notes, and provenance-like explanations.
The prose section compresses several of the predicates into human-facing design rules.
standouts: The file treats design consistency as a set of scoped invariants rather than loose guidelines.
It combines visual rules with narrative, motion, evidence, and artifact-consistency rules in the same ledger.
It distinguishes checker responsibility across predicate, build, and model checks.
It encodes design intent through both machine-facing predicates and plain-language messages.
conventions: Each predicate uses a stable kebab-case id.
Most predicate records follow id, applies_to, predicate, message, checker ordering.
Messages are written as short prescriptive explanations.
Predicate text uses symbols such as ≥, ≤, ∈, ≈, and arrows to express constraints compactly.
Status fields are optional and used for provenance or derivation notes.
The prose array uses complete sentence rules rather than structured objects.
concerns: Some predicates marked as machine-checkable are not locally specified enough to evaluate without external definitions.
Checker categories are not defined in this file, so the expected evaluator behavior is implicit.
Status strings mix provenance, verification state, and explanatory notes in one free-text field.
Several predicates reference external concepts or files without local schema definitions.
notes: This file is a design-invariant registry that turns brand, layout, motion, and storytelling principles into scoped checks and AI-readable guidance.
questions: What exact evaluator semantics distinguish predicate, build, and model checkers?
Which predicates are intended to be automatically testable versus reviewed by a model or human?
Should status metadata be structured into separate source, date, verification, and note fields?
What are the local definitions of terms like warm-metal family, movement layer, register, root, row, and semantic marks?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it provides machine-checkable-style invariants and prose guidance intended to hold across design dials and scales, though several checks are expressed in interpretive language.
apparent_themes: Narrative completeness at multiple reading grains
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to make a design system governable by giving both automated tools and AI reviewers explicit constraints, messages, and interpretive principles.
novelty: The file treats design, narrative structure, motion continuity, and artifact consistency as one invariant system rather than separating visual lint from content logic.
contribution: Contributes a structured set of scoped constraints and explanatory messages that a fused system would keep as the invariant ledger for visual discipline, narrative completeness, motion continuity, evidence handling, and purpose-aware judgment.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON invariant registry for counterpart-design defining scoped predicates, checker types, messages, and prose rules for color, typography, layout, motion, evidence, narrative completeness, and artifact consistency.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for making design principles checkable and explainable across generated artifacts, so tools and AI can evaluate whether outputs preserve the intended visual and narrative behavior.
